mission.title: Install and Configure Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP)
mission.description: Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) is an enterprise Kubernetes distribution that provides a developer-friendly platform for building and deploying applications. It includes features like integrated CI/CD, enhanced security, and a rich ecosystem of tools. Key trade-offs include the complexity of installation and management compared to simpler Kubernetes distributions.

steps.title: Install the OpenShift CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the OpenShift command-line interface (CLI) which is essential for managing your OpenShift cluster.

```bash

curl -LO https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/clients/ocp/latest/oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) && chmod +x oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) && sudo mv oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) /usr/local/bin/oc

```

> This command downloads the latest OpenShift CLI binary and installs it.
commands: curl -LO https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/clients/ocp/latest/oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) && chmod +x oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) && sudo mv oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) /usr/local/bin/oc

steps.title: Create an OpenShift cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new OpenShift cluster with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

openshift-install create cluster --dir=<installation_directory> --log-level=info

```

> Replace <installation_directory> with your desired directory for installation files.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created OpenShift cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=<installation_directory>/auth/kubeconfig

```

> Ensure to replace <installation_directory> with the same directory used in the previous step.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your OpenShift cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

oc get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-installation configuration
steps.description:
This step involves configuring autoscaling, monitoring, and RBAC for your OpenShift cluster.

```bash

oc apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openshift/cluster-autoscaler/master/deploy/cluster-autoscaler-operator.yaml

oc apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openshift/cluster-monitoring-operator/master/deploy/cluster-monitoring-config.yaml

```

> This command installs the Cluster Autoscaler.
> This command sets up monitoring for your cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the OpenShift cluster
uninstall.description:
This command will remove the OpenShift cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

openshift-install destroy cluster --dir=<installation_directory> --log-level=info

```

> Replace <installation_directory> with your installation directory.

uninstall.title: Clean up orphaned resources
uninstall.description:
This command helps to clean up any orphaned resources such as load balancers, PVCs, and DNS records.

```bash

kubectl delete pvc --all

```

> This command deletes all persistent volume claims.

upgrade.title: Backup the current cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your current cluster state.

```bash

oc adm must-gather --image=quay.io/openshift-release-dev/ocp-v4.0-art-dev:latest

```

> This command gathers diagnostic information.

upgrade.title: Drain and cordon nodes
upgrade.description:
This step prepares the nodes for upgrade by draining workloads and marking them as unschedulable.

```bash

oc adm drain <node_name> --ignore-daemonsets

```

> Replace <node_name> with the name of the node you want to drain.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the OpenShift cluster
upgrade.description:
This command upgrades the OpenShift cluster to the specified version.

```bash

openshift-install upgrade cluster --dir=<installation_directory> --log-level=info

```

> Ensure to replace <installation_directory> with your installation directory.

upgrade.title: Post-upgrade verification
upgrade.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes after the upgrade to ensure everything is functioning correctly.

```bash

oc get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: dial tcp <IP>:<PORT>: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the OpenShift API server is not reachable, possibly due to network issues or the server being down.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the OpenShift API server and ensure it is running. Use 'oc get pods -n openshift-apiserver' to verify.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image <image>: <error>'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs when the specified image cannot be found or accessed.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and ensure that the image registry is accessible from the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Node <node_name> is NotReady'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the node is not in a healthy state, possibly due to resource exhaustion or misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the node's resource usage and logs for any errors. Use 'oc describe node <node_name>' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU or memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources to schedule new pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up the cluster by adding more nodes or increasing the resources of existing nodes.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for Kubernetes 1.30, enhanced security features, and improved monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy authentication methods and removed support for certain older APIs.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 4.16, ensure to review the new security policies and update your configurations accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'oc get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' state, indicating that the cluster is operational.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/clients/ocp/latest/oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) && chmod +x oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) && sudo mv oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) /usr/local/bin/oc

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
